run_type: scheduled daily (Day +3 post-migration; 7 days since last Nhughes09-authored commit)
headline: Quiet day. Zero new external signals; zero dome infrastructure changes; three operator-hand items (GSC Domain property, Bing Webmaster, repo homepage field) remain pending and unchanged from 2026-04-23. The Nhughes09 author-commit drought is now 7 days — consistent with the 'one-off AI-steering burst on 2026-04-17' reading; nothing in that window has iterated. Our content-layer state is clean after yesterday's formula-drift landing (85.2% -> 83.1%); no open self-inflicted CLAUDE.md violation to chase. We are entirely operator-gated on actual discoverability progress.

operator_action_checklist.action: Verify funwithscience.net as a Google Search Console Domain property (DNS TXT via GoDaddy)
operator_action_checklist.rationale: Still the top blocker for new-domain indexing. Day +3 without verification. Cost of delay compounds — the dome site has had 7 full days of static content + hourly auto-commit churn and still has not been indexed, which is itself evidence that GitHub Pages properties do not get organically indexed without operator-driven submission. The same will hold for funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/ until GSC verification + sitemap submission happens.
operator_action_checklist.detected_signal: ls docs/google*.html -> no match; grep 'google-site-verification' docs/index.html -> no match. Unchanged since 2026-04-21 run2.
sub_actions_after_verify: Submit https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/sitemap.xml as a sitemap in the new property
URL Inspection push for https://funwithscience.net/ and https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/

operator_action_checklist.action: Bing Webmaster Tools verification on funwithscience.net
operator_action_checklist.rationale: DNS TXT retry was scheduled for 2026-04-22 per SOCIAL-NOTE-004 item 2 — now 2 days past the retry window. No BingSiteAuth.xml and no msvalidate meta tag have landed. Secondary blocker behind GSC.
operator_action_checklist.detected_signal: ls docs/BingSiteAuth.xml -> not present. grep msvalidate docs/index.html -> not present. No IndexNow hex-named .txt key file present. Unchanged.
operator_action_checklist.fallback_if_dns_retry_fails_again: Social can draft an HTML meta verification patch (msvalidate.01) for the decider to wire into build-scripts/generate-html.js. Not needed yet — awaiting operator result on the DNS retry path.

operator_action_checklist.action: Update GitHub repo homepage field for funwithscience-org/dome-model-review
operator_action_checklist.rationale: Public API confirms homepage still returns 'https://funwithscience-org.github.io/dome-model-review/' as of 2026-04-24 16:35 UTC. Cheap one-line fix; helps GitHub-repo-page SEO and resolves a live mismatch against every other canonical URL in the review's infrastructure.
operator_action_checklist.alternative: GitHub About panel edit in the repo web UI.
operator_action_checklist.detected_signal: curl https://api.github.com/repos/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review -> homepage still points at legacy URL. Unchanged.

operator_action_checklist.action: Validate ClaimReview JSON-LD at validator.schema.org against the new canonical URL
operator_action_checklist.rationale: Schema structure unchanged through migration; url and author.url correctly reference funwithscience.net, itemReviewed.url correctly retains john09289.github.io/predictions. Low-risk sanity check remains outstanding.
operator_action_checklist.detected_signal: No validator-reported errors observed, but the operator has not yet confirmed a live validator pass on the new canonical. No action from social — this is an interactive human check.

author_activity_search_notes: Web search surface remains null. 'john09289' OR 'Nhughes09' + dome/firmament/predictions -> zero dome-specific hits. 'ovoid cavity cosmological model' OR 'ECM dome model' flat earth 2026 -> returns only unrelated dome-themed content (journalofgeocentriccosmology.org, flatearth.ws/Walter Bislin, stock-image listings, Amazon/Etsy products). 'firmament resonance' OR 'aetheric refraction' OR '20015 km' dome cosmology -> returns ANIMA Framework and Journal of Geocentric Cosmology content, but these use DIFFERENT technical vocabulary (no Finsler coordinates, no ovoid cavity, no 20015/8537/5733 km numerical parameters, no 67/69-prediction structure). They are parallel dome/firmament communities, not derivative works. No third-party mention of funwithscience.net/dome-model-review. No review mentions anywhere.
claim_overlap_search_notes: No significant technical overlap detected. The adjacent communities surfaced in search (ANIMA Framework at animaframework.com, Journal of Geocentric Cosmology at journalofgeocentriccosmology.org, the 'Unified Dimensional Plasma Dome Layers 0D-18D' WordPress post) share generic firmament/dome concepts but none use the dome model's distinguishing features: no ovoid cavity geometry, no Finsler coordinates, no H(r) = 8537 * exp(-r/8619) firmament profile, no 20015 km disc radius, no 67/69-prediction taxonomy, no attribution to john09289. These remain parallel-but-unrelated cosmologies. We already noted ANIMA and JoGC in earlier runs; nothing new.

dome_repo.recent_commits_last_24h: 10+ (all scheduled-bot: 'Auto-update ECM status' hourly, 'auto: ECM data pull' periodic, 'auto: daily AI review' daily via Groq llama-3.3-70b)

repo_metadata.note: Unchanged — dome author still has not added any repo-level SEO metadata. 7+ days since the 2026-04-17 infrastructure burst.

machine_readable_fingerprints.note: All three fingerprints identical to 2026-04-23 baseline. No dome-side changes to counter.

dome_repo.notable: Seven full days of zero author-authored content changes. All activity is hourly/periodic/daily scheduled bots updating status JSON and regenerating daily_review.json. Confirms reading: 2026-04-17 was a one-off investment in AI-steering infrastructure, not the start of continuous iteration.

repo_metadata.description: Scientific critical review of the Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model (ECM) — every claim checked against published data

review_mentions_search_notes: Explicit 'funwithscience.net dome review' search returns zero on-topic hits (only Fun With Science education resources, Science Dome UK planetarium products, and Trustpilot reviews of fun-science.org.uk). No reference to our review in any indexed source. Still entirely pre-indexing.

discoverability.competitive_assessment: Day +3 of the post-migration window. Both sites remain unindexed by Google. Our relative position is structurally stronger than the dome's in every way that discoverability infrastructure matters: brandable short URL (funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/), SEO meta tags, ClaimReview JSON-LD, honest descriptive llms.txt reflecting accurate counts (71 wins / 69 dome-claimed with sub-entries / 83.1% honest accuracy vs 94.5% dome-claimed), populated repo description + topics, apex umbrella landing. The dome's AI-steering stack (llms.txt, ai_manifest.json, methodology.json, review-response.html) is static since 2026-04-17 with zero author iteration for 7 days — meaning the dome has NOT moved on any of its existing AI-facing infrastructure, and has NOT added any new infrastructure for us to counter. The gating factor for our own indexing progress is entirely operator-hand: GSC Domain property verification is the single highest-leverage action. Everything else in social's domain is downstream of that.

actions_taken.action: Appended 2026-04-24 run 1 to monitor/social/search-rankings.json (same 11-query panel as 2026-04-23 for trend continuity).
actions_taken.reasoning: Day +3 indexing check. Maintains the longitudinal trend — if any index begins to form we'll see it in position changes across the 11 tracked queries.

actions_taken.action: Re-verified all five autodetect signals (GSC, Bing, IndexNow, GitHub homepage, meta-tag verification). All five remain pending/unchanged from 2026-04-23. Did NOT re-flag any as a new issue — flagged only the three specific items remaining (GSC, Bing, homepage).
actions_taken.reasoning: Per SOCIAL-NOTE-003 directive, only flag items that are actually pending. Avoids the pre-2026-04-15 generic 'indexing strategy pending' phrasing that caused coordination gaps.

actions_taken.action: Re-verified our docs/llms.txt against current wins.json (71 entries, IDs 001-069 with two sub-entries), uncounted-failures.json (10 entries, dome_claimed_accuracy 94.5%, dome_claimed_failures 4, honest_accuracy = 83.1%). Our llms.txt correctly states '69 dome claims (V51.1) plus additional sub-entries' and the ClaimReview JSON-LD correctly says '69 confirmed predictions with 94.5% accuracy' (the dome's own claim, which is what ClaimReview is supposed to encode). No drafts needed this run — content is accurate.
actions_taken.reasoning: Phase 1 ownership rule: social reads but does not write docs/. Drafts only go out when content drifts from data/. Nothing drifted.

actions_taken.action: Re-verified that the sections.json formula landed correctly (part2b 'Monitoring Illusion' now uses template tokens {{UNCOUNTED_FAILURE_COUNT}} and {{INCL_FAILURES_ACCURACY_PCT}}, computed live to 10 and 83.1% respectively). grep '85.2' docs/index.html -> 0 matches; grep '83.1' -> 1 match. Closed loop confirmed.
actions_taken.reasoning: Sanity check on yesterday's landing. Social has been carrying this as open work since 2026-04-21; confirming the fix is durable across builds.

actions_taken.action: Re-verified dome machine-readable layer fingerprints. llms.txt md5 142bea6f... (3257 bytes), ai_manifest V51.1 (14880 bytes, md5 405d5c93...), sitemap.xml (2936 bytes, md5 929a345c..., all lastmods 2026-04-17). All identical to 2026-04-23 baseline.
actions_taken.reasoning: Dome stasis confirmed for another day. Baseline fingerprints unchanged — no new dome infrastructure to counter.

discoverability.issues_filed_note: No new issues filed this run. All four operator-hand items from 2026-04-23 remain open in checklist form (not decider-routable because they require human auth). No new build-pipeline gaps detected. No drift in docs/ content relative to data/ sources. Re-checked open-issues.json and closed-issues.json — 108 open issues exist, none social-owned and none that need re-filing.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has ai_manifest.json with ai_prompt_seed, agent_rules, and recommended_read_order designed to steer AI responses toward dome-favorable framings.
no_action_gaps.reasoning: AI-steering content (prompt-injection-adjacent patterns) is NOT something we should replicate. Our countermove is an honest, accurate, descriptive llms.txt that contradicts the dome's claims with verifiable evidence — not a counter-prompt-injection. We already have this. Continuing to not-match this pattern is the correct choice.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has a /api/ JSON endpoint (current/status, current/methodology, current/claim_index) that gives AI systems structured access to their model.
no_action_gaps.reasoning: We could mirror this with an /api/ endpoint serving our findings as JSON — but this has been discussed in prior reports as 'strategy not yet approved.' The current architecture (HTML with ClaimReview JSON-LD embedded) is the W3C standard for machine-readable fact-check data and is what search engines and fact-check aggregators actually consume. A custom /api/ path duplicates effort without a known consumer. Hold.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome site is git-commit-churning hourly via auto-update bots (100+ commits/24h) which may signal 'active project' to any crawler that weights update frequency.
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Matching this would be churn for its own sake and would dilute our signal-to-noise ratio with scheduled-agent commits. Our activity pattern (decider/analyst/curmudgeon commits on actual content changes) is what an honest project looks like. Declining to match.

files_verified_ok: docs/llms.txt (69 dome-claimed + sub-entries framing accurate; 94.5% vs our 83.1% accurate; Timestamp Error tab reference present; AI-Facing Read Path section present)
docs/index.html ClaimReview JSON-LD (url and author.url on funwithscience.net; itemReviewed.url correctly retains john09289.github.io/predictions)
docs/index.html verdict bars (71 total, reflecting current wins.json count)
data/sections.json part2b formula (template tokens in place, rendering 10 / 83.1%)

summary_of_strategic_position.our_advantage: Clean infrastructure, honest content, populated metadata, brandable URL, structural ClaimReview — all Phase-1-complete. Discoverability ceiling is capped by operator-hand items, not anything social or the build pipeline can address.
summary_of_strategic_position.dome_advantage: None that matters for search/indexing. AI-steering infrastructure is richer than ours but only helps if AI systems retrieve it — and without search-engine indexing, AI retrieval is improbable. Dome's lack of repo metadata and sitemap-absent-from-search-engines is a structural disadvantage relative to us.
summary_of_strategic_position.contrarian_read: Steelman the dome author's position: he may be deliberately avoiding search-engine submission on the theory that 'mainstream suppression' is part of the narrative, in which case zero-indexing IS the intended state. Under that read, our indexing push is the more exposed posture — we are committing to being evaluable. This is an acceptable trade for us (evaluation is our entire value proposition) but it's worth naming that the competing strategies are genuinely different goals, not failure-vs-success on the same goal.
what_would_change_the_picture: Operator completes GSC Domain property verification -> indexing timeline starts
Author resumes dev after the 7-day hiatus -> new dome infrastructure to analyze
Any third-party picks up the dome model in any forum/platform -> claim overlap or review mentions to track
Someone forks the dome repo -> signal of actual interest vs single-author isolation
